query: What are the different valid resource types for asset registration in SPP?
final_answer:
Below are the resource (asset) registration types SPP uses in the Integrated Marketplace (common names used in SPP protocols and guidance). Use the Market Protocols / registration UI for exact, up‑to‑date names and any subtypes:

- Generation Resource (conventional generator)  
- Electric Storage Resource (ESR) / Market Storage Resource (MSR) — energy storage participation model(s)  
- Dispatchable Demand Response Resource (DDR)  
- Block Demand Response Resource (BDR)  
- Intermittent/Variable Energy Resource (VER) — e.g., wind, solar (non‑dispatchable / forecasted)  
- Combined‑Cycle Resource (CC) — modeled with heat‑rate / mode logic (often a subtype of Generation)  
- Fuel‑Limited Resource (units with fuel constraints, e.g., limited energy/hydro)  
- Quick‑Start / Fast‑Start Resource (start characteristic based registration)  
- Self‑Scheduled / Non‑Market Resource (registered but not economically dispatched by SPP)  
- Unavailable / Offline / Start‑Up / Shut‑Down / Test statuses (registration/status categories used for resources not available for full market dispatch)  
- Distributed Energy Resource (DER) and Distributed Energy Resource Aggregation (DER Aggregator / DERA) — participation models added for Order No. 2222

Notes:
- SPP distinguishes resource “types” (ESR, DDR, BDR, VER, etc.) from resource “status” (Available, Unavailable, Self‑Scheduled, Start‑Up/Test, etc.). Both affect modeling and market participation.  
- Some items (e.g., Combined‑Cycle, Fuel‑Limited, Quick‑Start) are modeled as specific participation/qualification categories or subtypes within the Generation registration flow rather than wholly separate market participant classes.  
- SPP updates terminology and adds new types (e.g., MSR, DER aggregator) as protocols change — always confirm the current, authoritative list in the SPP Market Protocols (Registration / Resource Participation sections) or the SPP registration UI if you need the official canonical names.

SPP asset registration resource types list 'Asset Registration' 'resource type' SPP Integrated Marketplace 'resource type' registration
"Resource Type" "SPP" "Resource Type" list "Asset Registration" "Resource Types" SPP registration 'Resource Type' 'Asset' 'Registration'

steps.content:
Below are the resource (asset) registration types SPP uses in the Integrated Marketplace (common names used in SPP protocols and guidance). Use the Market Protocols / registration UI for exact, up‑to‑date names and any subtypes:

- Generation Resource (conventional generator)  
- Electric Storage Resource (ESR) / Market Storage Resource (MSR) — energy storage participation model(s)  
- Dispatchable Demand Response Resource (DDR)  
- Block Demand Response Resource (BDR)  
- Intermittent/Variable Energy Resource (VER) — e.g., wind, solar (non‑dispatchable / forecasted)  
- Combined‑Cycle Resource (CC) — modeled with heat‑rate / mode logic (often a subtype of Generation)  
- Fuel‑Limited Resource (units with fuel constraints, e.g., limited energy/hydro)  
- Quick‑Start / Fast‑Start Resource (start characteristic based registration)  
- Self‑Scheduled / Non‑Market Resource (registered but not economically dispatched by SPP)  
- Unavailable / Offline / Start‑Up / Shut‑Down / Test statuses (registration/status categories used for resources not available for full market dispatch)  
- Distributed Energy Resource (DER) and Distributed Energy Resource Aggregation (DER Aggregator / DERA) — participation models added for Order No. 2222

Notes:
- SPP distinguishes resource “types” (ESR, DDR, BDR, VER, etc.) from resource “status” (Available, Unavailable, Self‑Scheduled, Start‑Up/Test, etc.). Both affect modeling and market participation.  
- Some items (e.g., Combined‑Cycle, Fuel‑Limited, Quick‑Start) are modeled as specific participation/qualification categories or subtypes within the Generation registration flow rather than wholly separate market participant classes.  
- SPP updates terminology and adds new types (e.g., MSR, DER aggregator) as protocols change — always confirm the current, authoritative list in the SPP Market Protocols (Registration / Resource Participation sections) or the SPP registration UI if you need the official canonical names.
